title: Grammitis buesii
taxonomy: Grammitis buesii belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is a species of fern, specifically a member of the genus Grammitis. Grammitis buesii is a type of epiphytic fern, which means it grows on other plants for support. This species is often found in tropical and subtropical regions, and its classification is also sometimes referred to as a member of the genus Grammitis sect. Hymenophyllum. Its exact classification and relationship within the plant kingdom may be subject to further research and classification.

intro: Grammitis buesii, also known as Buesi's Grammitis or Grammitid fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Grammitis buesii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Grammitis buesii is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, and is also dried and processed into capsules and tablets for its purported health benefits.
